title: Voice and Movement I

prerequisites.text: Admission to this course is prioritised for students (a) registered for the Diploma and BA in Theatre & Performance (b) majoring in Theatre & Dance Studies.

outline: This course offers students an introduction to the key conceptual frames of performance: ACTOR/CHARACTER, SPACE, AUDIENCE, STORY. These conceptual frames are applied to a series of weekly case studies of different performance practices across select countries on the African continent. Students are also tutored through practical projects that explore these fundamental concepts in practice. A fieldwork component is included as part of this course.
lecture_times: Tuesday and Wednesday 3rd period. Tutorial Friday 3rd period. Practical on Tuesday afternoon.
dp_requirements: Attendance at 80% of lectures and 100% of tutorials, practicals and theatre visits, and satisfactory presentation of assignments are compulsory for DP certificate purposes.
assessment: Continuous assessment (essays, projects, practical tasks, class tests) counts 100%.
